blocks.content: Shirone guessed the situation.
Destroyer707 and his group had slightly altered Craner's trajectory with their concentrated fire.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: They came back to save me.
The angle was only about 10 degrees, but for Shirone in a state of extreme concentration, it was the only escape route.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Thanks to you…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You idiot!
Suddenly, Destroyer707's eyes widened, and he immediately turned and ran.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What are you doing? Run!
When Shirone looked back, the Tenmen who had returned to the starting point were firing their rifles.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh!
Limping on his numb leg, Shirone threw himself towards the exit.
As he crossed the boundary, the gunfire ceased.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's over. We're safe now.
While Death Princess sighed in relief, Shirone looked back at the Iron Homeland.
Tenman10, supported by his subordinates, was approaching the exit.

blocks.speaker: Tenman10
blocks.content: It's no use. The starting point has already changed. Even if they kill us, we'll just revive.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: You, Yahweh2.

Tenman10, who had stopped at the boundary, asked, "East Country or West Country? Whatever your affiliation, join our guild. We'll support you up to the Magnan level, and after that, guarantee 10% of the profits."

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Magnan means level 260. Are they serious? Supporting from level 1 would cost a fortune in galaxies.
The highest level he had reached before cleaning his codename was 183.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: I'm not interested in the Tenmen.
Tenman10 frowned.

blocks.speaker: Tenman10
blocks.content: What are you talking about? Of course, it's the main base. The Tenmen activities are just… never mind. Think carefully. Our guild's influence is among the top five in the homeland.
Destroyer707 and his group's expressions darkened.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: This is a threat.
Even large guilds rarely issue assassination orders to specific users.
There needs to be a justification, and if public opinion worsens, it becomes difficult to maintain the guild.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: But justification can be fabricated. Especially for someone like Yahweh2.
Just dodging bullets as a bio was enough to cause a scandal in the homeland.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No.
If you have a main base, you're even worse. Not revealing your guild's name is for that reason, right? Do whatever you want. I'll crush you.

blocks.speaker: Tenman10
blocks.content: You'll regret this.
Tenman10 had more to say, but High Gear stopped before he could.
As his body turned into a corpse and disappeared to the starting point, his subordinates retreated, pointing fingers.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Be careful. We'll remember your codename.
After the Tenmen disappeared from view, Shirone turned back to the Electronic Wasteland.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's go. We need to reach Atogram by tomorrow at least. Will you take me there?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, sure.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: I thought he was naive, but he's surprisingly bold.
Iron Homeland, Starting Point.
When Tenman10 opened his eyes, Sancho was frowning beside him.

blocks.speaker: Sancho
blocks.content: Boss.

blocks.speaker: Tenman10
blocks.content: …What happened?

blocks.speaker: Sancho
blocks.content: They left. I suggested scouting, but it didn't work. They're not afraid of assassination orders. Must be from a large guild.

blocks.speaker: Tenman10
blocks.content: Damn, how did one person break through? Rumors will spread soon, and it's embarrassing.

blocks.speaker: Sancho
blocks.content: That bastard headshotted me? Did you record everything? Report it to the operator immediately.

blocks.speaker: Tenman10
blocks.content: Will it be okay? The video can't be edited. Our voices will be in it too.

blocks.speaker: Sancho
blocks.content: Hmph! What can they do? But exploiting a system error is different. It's a permanent ban. He won't be able to set foot here again.

blocks.speaker: Tenman10
blocks.content: …Alright.
Tenman10 opened the operator suggestion page at the top of the augmented reality.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Do we have to spend the night in the Electronic Wasteland? Is Atogram that far?

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Hmm, is it far? It takes about three hours if you run, but if you're over level 100, it's a 30-minute cut. The problem is the low level.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: The problem is the height, not the distance. The end of the Electronic Wasteland is surrounded by a wall. To jump over it, High Gear's output needs to be at least level 10.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, so we need to level up as we go. Are there any prey here?

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Of course. Parts are hard to find, but in the beginning, you can earn a decent amount of galaxies. The standard is to save money here and buy a beginner's package in Atogram.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: I don't get it. Is this really your first time? How did you have the guts to stand up to Tenman10? If they make a kill list at their main base, it's over for all of us.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I told you, a friend is waiting for me in Atogram. He'll probably solve it.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: That dealer? What's his codename? If he's capable, I might know him too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yolga's Son.
The three turned their heads simultaneously.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What! Yolga's Son?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah. We don't get along well, but he's reliable in this area. Don't worry too much…

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What are you talking about! He's an East Country ranker! And he's ranked 4th in duels! Is that Yolga really your friend?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Probably… We're classmates, after all.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: So when we get to Atogram, can we meet him too? Will you introduce us?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course. I can't let you guys suffer because of me.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Suffer? This is rather…

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Not just any ranker, but Yolga's Son. This is huge. We must join East Country.
When he searched for Yolga's Son, the guild emblem filled the ranking section.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: East Country Guild Rank 1. Golden Wheel.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? Why is everyone grinning?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Huh? It's nothing. Anyway, I get it now. Is your goal to become a ranker too? Don't pretend you don't know me later.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Actually, I didn't come here to become a ranker. It's personal; I need to beat someone.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: That happens. A showdown in High Gear. So, your opponent is from the West Country, right? What's their codename? I'll look it up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Operator.
Silence fell.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There was no need to search, but his mind was in chaos.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is it a fake…

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: No, it's the real Operator. I've heard he's the strongest in High Gear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This guy really knows nothing. Otherwise, he wouldn't use such a common expression like 'the strongest.'

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Alright. Speaking as a veteran, your chances of beating Operator alone are zero. It's not that I'm underestimating you; it's just how High Gear's system works.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: As your level increases, so does your engine output. But that's the simplest part. When the AI level goes up, combat convenience improves significantly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if the levels are the same, the performance of parts varies greatly. And depending on how you set the selective options, the results can be completely different. Do you understand now? How long do you think it will take to match all these elements? Operator is an early user. That means he's one of the best machines High Gear can offer.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: His combat sense is also top-notch. Being undefeated in the duel rankings means that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This wasn't a world where gritting your teeth would get you anywhere.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But I can't give up. I will win. How do I meet Operator?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Alright, if you're really serious about it…
He discarded the empty magazine, inserted a new one into his rifle, and snapped it into place.
You have to hunt like crazy.
Operator's Meeting Room.
Twelve screens, each representing a department, were set up around the round table.
They were also mavericks of the undercoder, so they never revealed their real faces.
A system-related suggestion has come in…
The voice was modulated.
It's about what happened in the Iron Homeland. I'll play the recorded video, and then we can discuss it.
The video played simultaneously on all twelve screens.
Eliminate the blind spots! Don't aim at the target, shoot predictively into the space!
The video, recorded from Tenman10's perspective, showed Shirone struggling.
The operators remained silent.
Even after Shirone headshotted Sancho and the conversation at the boundary of the Iron Homeland ended.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Alright.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: It's not my specialty, but I thought we blocked all forms of magic. Did the firewall get breached?

blocks.speaker: Yo妖
blocks.content: No. There's no sign of infiltration or attack. The meta-process is also intact.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Then what is that? Is that a person? How did he dodge something faster than Mach?

blocks.speaker: Smile
blocks.content: According to the logs, it seems extreme time distortion occurred in a state of intense concentration. In other words, his neural transmission speed was faster than the bullet's speed…

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: So!

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: We blocked all magic and schema, so how is that possible? Is he a god or what?

blocks.speaker: Black Circle
blocks.content: The user's codename being Yahweh2 is also significant. Is it the Yahweh I know?

blocks.speaker: Gear
blocks.content: As the tutorial manager, I have no obligation to answer. However, I guarantee on my honor that he is not someone who would exploit system loopholes.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: What's with the serious tone? Who's Yahweh? Is it a person's name? Is he handsome?

blocks.speaker: Gear
blocks.content: Don't you know how the world works? Stop eating Dream Star and go for a walk.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Are you picking a fight with me?

blocks.speaker: Lollipop
blocks.content: Alright, enough! The review shows it's not a system error. However, there's room for interpretation based on judgment criteria. Our design motto is that all users are given equal opportunities in High Gear, right?

blocks.speaker: Yo妖
blocks.content: But we can't interfere with individual perceptions. Even in the same situation, reactions vary based on sensory differences. Denying Yahweh2's senses is denying the individuality of all existing users.

blocks.speaker: Smile
blocks.content: As long as it doesn't deviate from the design intent, it's fine. In fact, the Tenmen who reside in the Iron Homeland can't be said to adhere to High Gear's motto either.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: That's because it's fun. It's fun.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Anyway, since we're talking about design intent, what does our lady think?
Is there something you want to say, Number Seven?
